perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be construed in accordance with the laws of England and Wales, without giving effect to any choice of law rule, and
perturbation.changed_text: You will adhere to the Exam Supervision Guidelines and Exam Eligibility Guidelines as published from time to time by The Open Group at the certification site for the applicable program.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the choice of law provision to a completely irrelevant section undermines its enforceability. Choice of law should be easily located and clearly stated.
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 3 of the Unfair Contract Terms Act 1977 addresses attempts to exclude or restrict liability for breach of contract through unfair contract terms. By obscuring the choice of law provision, the contract becomes less clear about which jurisdiction's laws govern the agreement, increasing the risk of unfair or unenforceable terms being applied, which may violate the UCTA.

(1/4/1995) Back to Original Document << Previous TITLE 3 / CHAPTER 1 / § 3 Next >> [Print] [Print selection] [OLRC Home] Help 3 USC 3 : Number of electors Text contains those laws in effect on July 12, 2025 From Title 3-THE PRESIDENT CHAPTER 1-PRESIDENTIAL ELECTIONS AND VACANCIES Jump To: Source Credit §3. Number of electors The number of electors shall be equal to the number of Senators and Representatives to which the several States are by law entitled at the time when the President and Vice President to be chosen come into office; except, that where no apportionment of Representatives has been made after any enumeration, at the time of choosing electors, the number of electors shall be according to the then existing apportionment of Senators and Representatives. ( June 25, 1948, ch. 644, 62 Stat. 672 .)
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the contract will be construed in accordance with the laws of England and Wales. The changed text focuses on exam guidelines and the choice of law provision is undermined by being moved to an irrelevant section. This affects the clarity and enforceability of the contract. According to the definition of Structural Flaws - Legal Contradiction, a legal contradiction arises when a required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section, making it difficult for parties to locate or interpret. This is precisely what has happened here. Scraped Law Snippet 1 mentions Title 3 of the USC, which is not directly relevant, but the lack of a pertinent law snippet emphasizes the absence of legal grounding for the modified text's placement of the choice of law provision.

perturbation.original_text: You may not disclose the Exam questions or answers or discuss any of the content of the Exam Materials with any person, without prior written approval of The Open Group;
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement represents the entire Agreement of the parties hereto pertaining to the subject matter of this Agreement, and supersedes any and all prior oral discussions and/or written correspondence or agreements between the parties with respect thereto.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the confidentiality clause to a completely irrelevant section obscures its enforceability and diminishes its clarity. Confidentiality obligations should be prominently placed.
perturbation.law_explanation: Regulation 3 of the Trade Secrets (Enforcement, etc) Regulations 2018 defines a trade secret as information that is secret, has commercial value because it is secret, and has been subject to reasonable steps under the circumstances, by the person lawfully in control of the information, to keep it secret. By burying the non-disclosure clause, the Open Group may be seen as failing to take 'reasonable steps' to protect its exam materials, thus weakening its ability to claim them as trade secrets under UK law.

perturbation.justification: The change moves the confidentiality clause to an irrelevant section, diminishing its clarity and enforceability. The Law Explanation states this can lead to the Open Group failing to take 'reasonable steps' to protect its exam materials, potentially weakening their ability to claim them as trade secrets. This aligns with the Structural Flaws definition, where a required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section.
